compositorProperties.note: S-tier only when animated via CSS, WAAPI, or Motion. A-tier when driven by JS/rAF.

svgAttributes.note: SVG shape attributes trigger repaint every frame. Use transform for position/scale instead.

layoutTriggeringReads.note: DOM APIs that force synchronous layout when called after a pending style write. Reading these in a loop with writes = F-tier thrashing.

motionPropertyMap.note: Motion shorthand props and their CSS equivalents. All map to compositor properties but cannot be hardware accelerated (A-tier via rAF).

layoutProps.note: These Motion props trigger A-tier transform animations but require upfront measurements, making them B-tier.

motion.note: Uses WAAPI for compositor properties. layout/layoutId = B-tier.

gsap.note: Uses requestAnimationFrame, not WAAPI. A-tier ceiling for compositor properties. GSAP's Flip is B-tier.

lottie.note: Canvas/SVG rendering. Canvas = C-tier (paint). SVG = C-tier (repaint per frame).

antiPatterns.name: Layout thrashing
antiPatterns.detection: Interleaved DOM reads (offset*, client*, scroll*, getBoundingClientRect, getComputedStyle) and writes (style.*, className, classList, setAttribute) in loops or rAF callbacks
antiPatterns.fix: Batch all reads before writes. Use requestAnimationFrame or Motion frame.read()/frame.update().

antiPatterns.name: CSS variable inheritance bomb
antiPatterns.detection: Animating CSS custom properties on :root, html, or body elements (document.documentElement.style.setProperty, document.body.style.setProperty with '--')
antiPatterns.fix: Scope variable to smallest subtree. Use @property with inherits: false. Animate target property directly.

antiPatterns.name: scrollTop animation driver
antiPatterns.detection: Reading scrollTop, scrollLeft, scrollY, pageYOffset inside scroll event listener or rAF loop to drive animation values
antiPatterns.fix: Use CSS scroll-timeline, position: sticky/fixed, or Motion scroll() which uses native ScrollTimeline.

antiPatterns.name: CSS variable in compositor value
antiPatterns.detection: CSS variable (var(--*)) used as or within value for transform, opacity, filter, or clip-path
antiPatterns.fix: Animate the property directly for S or A-tier animation. At least register with @property { inherits: false } to reduce repaint cascade.

antiPatterns.name: Excessive will-change
antiPatterns.detection: will-change with >10 properties, or will-change applied to >10 elements simultaneously, or will-change: auto on animated elements
antiPatterns.fix: Limit will-change to 1-2 properties on elements currently animating. Remove after animation completes.

antiPatterns.name: Large blur layer
antiPatterns.detection: filter: blur() with value >10px on elements wider/taller than 500px or full-viewport elements
antiPatterns.fix: Reduce blur radius. Apply blur to a smaller element. Use a pre-blurred image instead.

antiPatterns.name: Off-screen animation running
antiPatterns.detection: Continuous animations (infinite, long duration >5s) without IntersectionObserver or whileInView to pause when off-screen
antiPatterns.fix: Use IntersectionObserver or Motion inView/whileInView to deactivate off-screen animations.

antiPatterns.name: Missing prefers-reduced-motion
antiPatterns.detection: Movement (top/left etc or transform) code present but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion) in stylesheets and no matchMedia('prefers-reduced-motion') in JS, or useReducedMotion hook used without fallback
antiPatterns.fix: Wrap tree in <MotionConfig reducedMotion='user'> or use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) with meaningful alternatives (not just animation: none).

antiPatterns.name: Multiple DOM-reading libraries
antiPatterns.detection: Two or more animation libraries imported that independently schedule DOM reads/writes (e.g., GSAP + react-spring, or any two non-Motion libraries)
antiPatterns.fix: Use Motion frame.read()/frame.update() as shared scheduler, or consolidate to one library.

antiPatterns.name: View Transition instant interruption
antiPatterns.detection: document.startViewTransition without handling concurrent navigation or state changes
antiPatterns.fix: Queue or batch view transitions. Motion's animateView handles interruption automatically.
